# Hook: Pre-Commit

## Identity
This hook fires automatically before every `git commit`. It is the first line of defense against committing broken, insecure, or non-compliant code.

**If this hook fails, the commit is blocked. No exceptions.**

---

## Trigger
```bash
# .git/hooks/pre-commit (generated by husky or manual install)
# Or configured in .github/hooks/pre-commit.sh
```

## Execution Sequence

Every commit runs these checks in order. A failure at any step blocks the commit:

### Check 1: Secret Detection (CRITICAL — always first)
```bash
echo "🔍 Scanning for secrets..."

# Run truffleHog on staged files only (fast)
git stash -q --keep-index
trufflehog filesystem --directory . \
  --include-paths <(git diff --cached --name-only) \
  --fail
RESULT=$?
git stash pop -q

if [ $RESULT -ne 0 ]; then
  echo "🚨 BLOCKED: Potential secrets detected in staged files"
  echo "   Run 'trufflehog filesystem .' to see details"
  echo "   Remove secrets and use environment variables / Azure Key Vault"
  exit 1
fi

echo "✅ Secret scan passed"
```

### Check 2: Build Verification
```bash
echo "🔨 Verifying build..."

dotnet build --no-incremental --configuration Debug 2>&1
if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
  echo "🚫 BLOCKED: Build failed. Fix compilation errors before committing."
  exit 1
fi

echo "✅ Build passed"
```

### Check 3: Unit Tests (fast tests only)
```bash
echo "🧪 Running unit tests..."

dotnet test --filter "Category!=Integration&Category!=E2E&Category!=Performance" \
  --no-build \
  --logger "console;verbosity=minimal" \
  2>&1

if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
  echo "🚫 BLOCKED: Unit tests failed. All unit tests must pass before committing."
  exit 1
fi

echo "✅ Unit tests passed"
```

### Check 4: Framework Dependency Check
```bash
echo "🔎 Checking for Framework dependencies in migrated files..."

# Check staged .cs files for banned namespaces
STAGED_CS=$(git diff --cached --name-only | grep "\.cs$")

if [ -n "$STAGED_CS" ]; then
  VIOLATIONS=$(echo "$STAGED_CS" | xargs grep -l "using System\.Web" 2>/dev/null)
  if [ -n "$VIOLATIONS" ]; then
    echo "🚫 BLOCKED: System.Web references found in:"
    echo "$VIOLATIONS"
    echo "   Remove System.Web dependencies before committing migrated files."
    exit 1
  fi
  
  # Check for ConfigurationManager in non-legacy files
  CONFIG_VIOLATIONS=$(echo "$STAGED_CS" | xargs grep -l "ConfigurationManager\." 2>/dev/null | \
    grep -v "Legacy\|Framework\|Compat")
  if [ -n "$CONFIG_VIOLATIONS" ]; then
    echo "⚠️  WARNING: ConfigurationManager found (should use IConfiguration) in:"
    echo "$CONFIG_VIOLATIONS"
    # Warning only — not blocking (may be legitimate legacy file)
  fi
fi

echo "✅ Framework dependency check passed"
```

### Check 5: Code Style (EditorConfig / dotnet format)
```bash
echo "🎨 Checking code formatting..."

dotnet format --verify-no-changes --no-restore 2>&1
if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
  echo "🚫 BLOCKED: Code formatting violations found."
  echo "   Run 'dotnet format' to auto-fix, then re-stage."
  exit 1
fi

echo "✅ Code format check passed"
```

### Check 6: Vulnerable Package Check
```bash
echo "📦 Checking for vulnerable packages..."

# Only run if .csproj files changed
CSPROJ_CHANGED=$(git diff --cached --name-only | grep "\.csproj$")
if [ -n "$CSPROJ_CHANGED" ]; then
  VULN_OUTPUT=$(dotnet list package --vulnerable 2>&1)
  if echo "$VULN_OUTPUT" | grep -q "has the following vulnerable packages"; then
    echo "🚨 BLOCKED: Vulnerable NuGet packages found:"
    echo "$VULN_OUTPUT"
    echo "   Update vulnerable packages before committing."
    exit 1
  fi
fi

echo "✅ Package vulnerability check passed"
```

### Check 7: Large File Detection
```bash
echo "📏 Checking for oversized files..."

# Block commits of files > 1MB (except known large files)
LARGE_FILES=$(git diff --cached --name-only | while read f; do
  SIZE=$(git cat-file -s ":$f" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
  if [ $SIZE -gt 1048576 ]; then
    echo "$f ($SIZE bytes)"
  fi
done)

if [ -n "$LARGE_FILES" ]; then
  echo "🚫 BLOCKED: Files exceeding 1MB detected:"
  echo "$LARGE_FILES"
  echo "   Use Git LFS for large binary files."
  exit 1
fi

echo "✅ File size check passed"
```

---

## Bypass (Emergency Only)

```bash
# ONLY for documented emergencies — always requires follow-up ticket
git commit --no-verify -m "EMERGENCY: [ticket #] [reason] — bypass justification"

# This creates an automatic alert to agent-security-audit
# and creates a tech-debt ticket that MUST be resolved within 48h
```

---

## Configuration

```yaml
# .pre-commit-config.yaml (using pre-commit framework)
repos:
  - repo: local
    hooks:
      - id: secret-scan
        name: Secret Detection
        entry: trufflehog filesystem .
        language: system
        pass_filenames: false
        
      - id: dotnet-build
        name: .NET Build
        entry: dotnet build --no-incremental
        language: system
        pass_filenames: false
        
      - id: dotnet-test-unit
        name: Unit Tests
        entry: dotnet test --filter "Category!=Integration"
        language: system
        pass_filenames: false
        
      - id: dotnet-format
        name: Code Format
        entry: dotnet format --verify-no-changes
        language: system
        pass_filenames: false
```

---

## Agent Notification on Failure

When this hook fails due to security issues, automatically notify:

```
TO: agent-security-audit
TRIGGER: pre-commit-secret-detected
COMMIT_AUTHOR: [git config user.email]
BRANCH: [current branch]
FILES: [list of files with violations]
ACTION_REQUIRED: Review and confirm secrets are removed from history
```
